rules:
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Benched Pokémon V and Benched Pokémon-GX.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

The Pokémon this card is attached to can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

The Pokémon VMAX this card is attached to gets -100 HP, and if it is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card. You can't attach this card to a Pokémon VMAX that has 100 HP or less remaining.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

The Basic Pokémon this card is attached to gets +50 HP, except Pokémon-GX.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

flavorText: It knows the forest inside and out. If it comes across a wounded Pokémon, Oranguru will gather medicinal herbs to treat it.

attacks.name: Obstruct
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

rules:
Flip 2 coins. Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of heads, put them into your hand, and shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Put up to 4 in any combination of Water Pokémon and Water Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

During this turn, your Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

If you go first, you may play this card during your first turn.

Draw 2 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Discard the top 5 cards of your deck, and attach any Energy cards you discarded in this way to your Benched Fighting Pokémon in any way you like.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 3 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, discard up to 3 cards from your hand. (You must discard at least 1 card.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.effect: You may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Water Energy.

Prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to the Water Pokémon this card is attached to. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Fighting Energy.

The Fighting Pokémon this card is attached to takes 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Metal Energy.

The Metal Pokémon this card is attached to has no Weakness.

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Grass Energy.

The Grass Pokémon this card is attached to recovers from all Special Conditions and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

Whenever either player plays a Pokémon VMAX from their hand to evolve a Pokémon V during their turn, heal 100 damage from that Pokémon.

This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

attacks.name: Energy Cutoff
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Loop Cannon
attacks.effect: Put 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Known for forming harmonious couples, this Pokémon is brought to wedding ceremonies as a good luck charm.
From its mouth, it fires the seeds of berries it has eaten. The scattered seeds give rise to new plants.
It may look spindly, but its neck muscles are heavy-duty. It can peck at a tree 16 times per second!

attacks.name: Jet Draft
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Tornadus expels massive energy from its tail, causing severe storms. Its power is great enough to blow houses away.

attacks.name: Max Glide
attacks.effect: You may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

attacks.name: Amazing Burst
attacks.effect: Discard all basic Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each type of basic Energy you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It flies in the ozone layer, way up high in the sky. Until recently, no one had ever seen it.

attacks.name: Round
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

flavorText: Its roar in battle shakes the ground like a tremor—or like an earthquake has struck.

attacks.name: Round
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

flavorText: The shock waves from its cries can tip over trucks. It stamps its feet to power up.

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If it senses danger, it scares the foe by crying out with the volume of a jet-plane engine.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It dives at a steep angle as soon as it spots its prey. It catches its prey with sharp claws.

attacks.name: Double Peck
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It dislikes cold seasons. They migrate to other lands in search of warmth, flying over 180 miles a day.

attacks.name: Wind Pressure
attacks.effect: If your opponent has 5 or fewer cards in their hand, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.
It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

attacks.name: Raging Claws
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body resembles polished metal, and it's both lightweight and strong. The only drawback is that it rusts easily.

attacks.name: Overhaul
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Windup Cannon
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Built roughly 500 years ago by a scientist, the part called the Soul-Heart is the actual life-form.

attacks.name: Max Hack
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card you have taken.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Grip and Squeeze
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Living in mud with a high iron content has given it a strong steel body.

attacks.name: Swift Swing
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon scrapes its spikes across rocks, and then uses the tips of its feelers to absorb the nutrients it finds within the stone.

attacks.name: Reaction
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It defends itself by launching spikes, but its aim isn't very good at first. Only after a lot of practice will it improve.

attacks.name: Drill Run
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It's not uncommon for tunnels that appear to have formed naturally to actually be a result of Excadrill's rampant digging.

attacks.name: Rewind Time
attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 Metal Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flash of Destruction
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

attacks.name: Spike Draw
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Knuckle Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It controls waves known as auras, which are powerful enough to pulverize huge rocks. It uses these waves to take down its prey.

attacks.name: Amazing Star
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 7 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Once every 1,000 years, the singing of a pure voice will rouse this Pokémon from its near-perpetual slumber. It wakes for only seven days.

attacks.name: Leg Quake
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It analyzes its opponents with more accuracy than a supercomputer, which enables it to calmly back them into a corner.
It flies at high speeds around the skies. When it finds its prey, Metang takes a firm grip with its sharp claws and never lets go.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If you anger it, it will do more than rampage. It will also burst out strong magnetism, causing nearby machines to break.

attacks.name: Raging Hammer
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Iron Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Double KO
attacks.effect: Both Active Pokémon are Knocked Out.

flavorText: In the moment that it gulps down its prey, the inside of its shell is exposed, but to this day, no one has ever seen that sight.

attacks.name: Stealy Claws
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. If any of them are heads, your opponent reveals their hand. Then, for each heads, discard a Trainer card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Living with a savage, seafaring people has toughened this Pokémon's body so much that parts of it have turned to iron.

attacks.name: Trash Cyclone
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those cards into your deck.

attacks.name: Poison Spray
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: This Pokémon eats trash, which turns into poison inside its body. The main component of the poison depends on what sort of trash was eaten.

attacks.name: Lucky Find
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its favorite places are unsanitary ones. If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.

attacks.name: Dredge Up
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Tantrum
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It conceals itself in sandstorms that Flygon whip up and waits patiently for prey to appear.

attacks.name: Dredge Up
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It buries some of its prey in the sand to use as emergency meals when its hunts are unsuccessful.

attacks.name: Dredge Up
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Sandile's still not good at hunting, so it mostly eats things that have collapsed in the desert. It's called "the cleaner of the desert."

attacks.name: Hazardous Claws
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Filch
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

flavorText: This Pokémon is feared. When its gemstone eyes begin to glow with a sinister shine, it's believed that Sableye will steal people's spirits away.

attacks.name: Ferocious Bellow
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 50 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It chases down prey in a pack of around ten. They defeat foes with perfectly coordinated teamwork.
It has a very tenacious nature. Its acute sense of smell lets it chase a chosen prey without ever losing track.

attacks.name: Metal Armament
attacks.effect: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Amazing Shield
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon VMAX.

flavorText: Its ability to deflect any attack led to it being known as the Fighting Master's Shield. It was feared and respected by all.

attacks.name: Full Nelson
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Tentacle Buster
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon used Full Nelson during your last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.
It's very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

attacks.name: Eruption Shot
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is an Energy card, this attack does 90 more damage, and attach that card to this Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Heavy Slam
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 less damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: Mud that hardens around a Mudsdale's legs sets harder than stone. It's so hard that it allows this Pokémon to scrap a truck with a single kick.
Loads weighing up to 50 times as much as its own body weight pose no issue for this Pokémon. It's skilled at making use of mud.
They live alone without forming packs. They will only listen to orders from Trainers who can draw out their true power.
When it rubs the rocks on its neck against you, that's proof of its love for you. However, the rocks are sharp, so the gesture is quite painful!

attacks.name: Core Avenger
attacks.effect: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Born when all of Zygarde's cells have been gathered together, it uses force to neutralize those who harm the ecosystem.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Earthen Power
attacks.effect: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It has phenomenal power. It will mercilessly crush anyone or anything that bullies small Pokémon.

attacks.name: Focus Fist
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It brings its claws together and whirls around at high speed before rushing toward its prey.

attacks.name: Best Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It's exceedingly energetic, with enough stamina to keep running all through the night. Taking it for walks can be a challenging experience.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Megaton Fall
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: The same rocks that form its body have been found in ground layers around the world.

attacks.name: Cycle Draw
attacks.effect: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Tornado Kick
attacks.effect: If you played Bea from your hand during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The longer and bigger its tusks, the higher its rank in its herd. The tusks take long to grow.

attacks.name: Strike Back
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is strong despite its compact size. It can easily pick up and carry an adult human on its back.

attacks.name: Deck Distiller
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It stores berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.

attacks.name: Fickle Impact
attacks.effect: If you have exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It has an easygoing nature. It doesn't care if it bumps its head on boats and boulders while swimming.
This Pokémon lives in cold water. It will leave the water to search for food when it gets cold outside.

attacks.name: Metal Armament
attacks.effect: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Amazing Sword
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Pokémon VMAX in play, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: Now armed with a weapon it used in ancient times, this Pokémon needs only a single strike to fell even Gigantamax Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wonder Shine
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it's decorated with cream.

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

attacks.name: Sensitive Ray
attacks.effect: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.

attacks.name: Geo Hunt
attacks.effect: Put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Aurora Gain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When the horns on its head shine in seven different colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.

attacks.name: Tail Trickery
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its upper whiskers are sensors that survey its surroundings. Its lower whiskers are organs that shoot electricity.

attacks.name: Triple Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Flying Fury
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, you may discard any number of Pokémon Tools from your Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it'll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.
It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.
Emitting powerful sound waves tires it out. Afterward, it won't be able to fly for a little while.

attacks.name: Unamplified Soundwave
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If you have any cards in your hand, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: While inside a cave, if you look up and see lots of heart-shaped marks lining the walls, it's evidence that Woobat live there.

attacks.name: Auspicious Tone
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Pokémon and a Supporter card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Emitting ultrasonic cries, it floats on winds to travel great distances.
At the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away. No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its body is entirely hollow. When it opens its mouth, it sucks everything in as if it were a black hole.

attacks.name: Future Sight
attacks.effect: Look at the top 4 cards of either player's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: If it finds bad children who won't listen to their parents, it will spirit them away—or so it's said.
Resentment at being cast off made it spring into being. Some say that treating it well will satisfy it, and it will once more become a stuffed toy.

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.effect: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: There's a proverb that says, "Shun the house where Shuppet gather in the growing dusk."

attacks.name: Life Squeeze
attacks.effect: Put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.

flavorText: A most peculiar Pokémon that somehow appears in a Poké Ball when a Nincada evolves.

attacks.name: Psypower
attacks.effect: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Commanding Tail
attacks.effect: You may have your opponent shuffle their hand into their deck. If you do, your opponent draws 4 cards.

flavorText: Its tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent by biting.
A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

attacks.name: Mini-Metronome
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

flavorText: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

attacks.name: Spinning Fan
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

attacks.name: Fighting Lightning
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V or Pokémon-GX, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It runs as fast as lightning strikes, shredding its opponents with its high-voltage claws.

attacks.name: Wild Shock
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When the interior part of its tail spins like a motor, Zekrom can generate many bolts of lightning to blast its surroundings.

attacks.name: Electrified Bite Mark
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if they attach an Energy card from their hand to the Defending Pokémon, put 6 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electro Sprinkler
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon and 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

attacks.name: Shocking Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.
One alone can emit only trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

attacks.name: Stun Needle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Shocking Pursuit
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could be in for three full days and nights of paralysis.
Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It's soaking up static electricity because it can't produce a charge on its own.
They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.
Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.
It stimulates its own muscles with electricity, so it can move quickly. It eases its soreness with electricity, too, so it can recover quickly as well.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

attacks.name: Amazing Shot
attacks.effect: This attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This rough Pokémon stores energy inside its body, then sweeps across the land, shooting off electricity.

attacks.name: Dazzle Blast
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Damaging Spark
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Thunder Snipe
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 160 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon V or Pokémon-GX. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.
If it is angered or startled, the fur all over its body bristles like sharp needles that pierce foes.
It stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.
Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

attacks.name: G-Max Volt Tackle
attacks.effect: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 150 more damage.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Targeted Skewer
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon for each damage counter on that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This Pokémon has a jaw that's as sharp as a spear and as strong as steel. Apparently Barraskewda's flesh is surprisingly tasty, too.

attacks.name: Flock
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Arrokuda and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If it sees any movement around it, this Pokémon charges for it straightaway, leading with its sharply pointed jaw. It's very proud of that jaw.

attacks.name: Continuous Gulp Missile
attacks.effect: Discard any number of Arrokuda from your Bench. This attack does 60 damage for each Arrokuda you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Cramorant's gluttony led it to try to swallow an Arrokuda whole, which in turn led to Cramorant getting an Arrokuda stuck in its throat.

attacks.name: Vise Wave
attacks.effect: If you played Nessa from your hand during this turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: With jaws that can shear through steel rods, this highly aggressive Pokémon chomps down on its unfortunate prey.

attacks.name: Jaw Lock
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Apparently the itch of its teething impels it to snap its jaws at anything in front of it.

attacks.name: Max Whiteout
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Freezing Headbutt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Frozen Slice
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aqua Wash
attacks.effect: You may put 2 Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: One swing of the sword incorporated in its armor can fell an opponent. A simple glare from one of them quiets everybody.

attacks.name: Aqua Wash
attacks.effect: You may put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: As a result of strict training, each Dewott learns different forms for using the scalchops.
It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can sometimes knock out opponents with the shock created by breaching and crashing its big body onto the water.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It shows off by spraying jets of seawater from the nostrils above its eyes. It eats a solid ton of Wishiwashi every day.
When Vaporeon's fins begin to vibrate, it is a sign that rain will come within a few hours.

attacks.name: Fast Flight
attacks.effect: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Discard your hand and draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Bright Wing
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is as hot as lava and is always billowing. Flames will occasionally burst from its shell.

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn't keep moving, its body will cool and harden.
Once it has stored up enough heat, this Pokémon's body temperature can reach up to 1,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Royal Blaze
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Leon card in your discard pile.

flavorText: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

attacks.name: Raging Flames
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

flavorText: It has a barbaric nature. In battle, it whips its fiery tail around and slashes away with sharp claws.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Jungle Rising
attacks.effect: You may attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, heal all damage from that Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mysterious Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Special Anchor
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: They inhabit mountainous regions. The leader of the herd is decided by a battle of clashing horns.

attacks.name: Synthesis
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If it has sunshine and water, it doesn't need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Techno Blast
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flower Bearing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon and all attached cards and puts them on the bottom of their deck.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its cry leaves a lasting headache if heard for too long. It moves so quickly that it is almost invisible.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Because it lived almost entirely underground, it is nearly blind. It uses its antennae instead.

attacks.name: Fan Tornado
attacks.effect: You may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe's mind and take preemptive action.
It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.
If it remains still, it looks just like a real nut. It delights in surprising foraging Pokémon.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Amazing Bloom
attacks.effect: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon has the ability to move through time. Records describing it as a forest deity can be found from many different eras.

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

flavorText: It sticks tree bark to itself with its saliva, making itself thicker and larger. Elderly Pineco are ridiculously huge.

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.

attacks.name: Head Crack
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

flavorText: Each of its three heads has its own thoughts. When they want to go in different directions, Exeggutor becomes unable to move.
Six of them form a single Pokémon. Should one of the six be lost, the next morning there will once more be six.
May appear in a swarm. Flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.

attacks.name: Shed Skin
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

attacks.name: Bug Hunch
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Grass Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its poison stinger is very powerful. Its bright-colored body is intended to warn off its enemies.
